transcript: The smoke, it gets your attention. You can't help but wonder what's going on where that smoke is coming from. Barbecue isn't about concocting some rub that is your secret ingredient. The pitmasters are willing to tell you everything that they do to it and then they'll sit back and be like well good luck. Tootsie's been cooking for over 50 years. She is one of the hardest working people I've ever met. I can't tell you how hot my pits are because I feel them with my hands. It's just something I do. Lennox uses fire to cook lettuce. He uses fire to cook pastry. That's crazy. Cooking with fire, it's something that has a life because you're never fully in control. The fire's in control. Rosalia still cooking like the Mayas did a thousand years ago. This is where barbecue started. Whole hog barbecue. It's not something he learned from a book. Rodney lived it. That aroma's always in the air. People just pour right in. Family. Friends. Sometimes strangers. Some of them, I'll never see them again. But I'm happy that I've made them happy. That's barbecue.
